description: Talos Kubernetes cluster health across every compute dimension: nodes, workloads, CPU/memory saturation, network, storage, GPUs, and control plane. Metrics via OTEL kubeletstats + k8sclusterreceiver + DCGM + istiod.

panels.description: Nodes reporting Ready=1 out of total nodes in the cluster.
panels.title: Nodes Ready

panels.description: Pods in phase Running (k8s_pod_phase == 2).
panels.title: Running Pods

panels.description: Pods in phase Pending (k8s_pod_phase == 1). Stuck scheduling, image pull, or PVC bind.
panels.title: Pending Pods

panels.description: Pods in phase Failed (k8s_pod_phase == 4). Investigate restart policy / exit codes.
panels.title: Failed Pods

panels.description: Total CPU cores in use across the cluster (sum of per-node kubelet CPU usage). Node capacity isn't exported by kubeletstats, so this is absolute cores, not a percentage.
panels.title: Cluster CPU

panels.description: Memory used / (used + available) averaged across nodes.
panels.title: Cluster Memory

panels.description: Average GPU utilization across all DCGM-reporting GPUs.
panels.title: Cluster GPU

panels.description: New container restarts in the last hour (increase on k8s_container_restarts).
panels.title: Restarts (1h)

panels.description: Sum of available deployment replicas across namespaces. Drop below desired = rollout stalled.
panels.title: Deployments

panels.description: StatefulSet pods ready vs desired.
panels.title: StatefulSets

panels.description: DaemonSet nodes ready vs desired. Misscheduled > 0 flags a problem.
panels.title: DaemonSets

panels.description: Active jobs, plus failed job pods — investigate any non-zero failure count.
panels.title: Jobs

panels.description: Running pod distribution across namespaces.
panels.title: Running Pods by Namespace

panels.description: All pods grouped by lifecycle phase (Pending/Running/Succeeded/Failed/Unknown).
panels.title: Pods by Phase

panels.description: Per-node health snapshot: readiness, CPU, memory, filesystem, pod density. One row per node.
panels.title: Nodes Overview

panels.description: Per-node CPU cores in use (kubelet CPU usage).
panels.title: Node CPU (cores used)

panels.description: Per-node memory used / total.
panels.title: Node Memory Utilization

panels.description: Per-node root filesystem usage as percent of capacity.
panels.title: Node Filesystem %

panels.description: Aggregate pod network throughput rolled up per node (rx + tx).
panels.title: Node Network (pod aggregate)

panels.description: Count of pods in each phase over time. Running should dominate; Pending/Failed spikes indicate scheduling or crash issues.
panels.title: Pods by Phase (over time)

panels.description: Containers with the most restarts in the last hour. Hot spots for crash loops and OOMs.
panels.title: Top Restarting Containers (1h)

panels.description: Deployment available vs desired per namespace. Gap > 0 means at least one deployment is under-replicated.
panels.title: Deployments — Desired vs Available

panels.description: HorizontalPodAutoscaler current vs desired replicas by namespace. Persistent mismatch = throttled or waiting on pods.
panels.title: HPA — Current vs Desired Replicas

panels.description: Top N pods by CPU usage (cores used).
panels.title: Top Pods — CPU

panels.description: Top N pods by memory usage (bytes).
panels.title: Top Pods — Memory (working set)

panels.description: Containers using >70% of their memory limit — OOMKill candidates.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (service_name, service_namespace) (k8s_pod_memory_working_set{service_namespace=~"$namespace"}) / on (service_name, service_namespace) group_left() sum by (service_name, service_namespace) (k8s_container_memory_limit{service_namespace=~"$namespace"}) > 0.5)

panels.title: Memory Pressure — Pods Near Limit

panels.description: CPU cores requested vs used per namespace. Large gaps = over-reserved capacity.
panels.title: CPU: Used vs Requested (per namespace)

panels.description: Memory working-set vs requested bytes per namespace.
panels.title: Memory: Used vs Requested (per namespace)

panels.description: Top pods by ingress (receive) rate.
panels.title: Top Pods — Network RX

panels.description: Top pods by egress (transmit) rate.
panels.title: Top Pods — Network TX

panels.description: Pods with non-zero network error rate (rx+tx errors). Healthy baseline is 0.
panels.title: Pod Network Errors

panels.description: Top pods by ephemeral filesystem bytes used. Growing rapidly = log rot / missing rotation / leak.
panels.title: Top Pods — Ephemeral Filesystem

panels.description: Node filesystem usage trend. Trajectory matters more than the value.
panels.title: Node Filesystem — Trend

panels.description: Per-GPU SM utilization. Sustained high means the GPU is the bottleneck; idle means capacity to schedule more work.
panels.title: GPU Utilization

panels.description: Framebuffer memory used per GPU (MiB).
panels.title: GPU Memory

panels.description: GPU die temperature. NVIDIA thermal throttle typically starts ~87°C.
panels.title: GPU Temperature

panels.description: Instantaneous GPU power draw in watts.
panels.title: GPU Power

panels.description: Workload intensity: tensor-core and SM pipelines active fraction. Tensor active correlates with FP16/BF16/FP8 matmul heavy workloads.
panels.title: GPU Compute Pipeline Activity

panels.description: Pods currently scheduled on each GPU. Memory figures are per-GPU totals, not per-pod.
panels.title: Pods on GPU

panels.description: xDS pushes per second by type. Sustained high = churn in config/endpoints.
panels.title: xDS Pushes

panels.description: p50 / p95 / p99 proxy convergence time. Spikes mean data plane is slow to pick up config.
panels.title: Proxy Convergence Time

panels.description: Pilot discovery state: registered services, virtual services, and managed clusters.
panels.title: Istio Discovery

panels.description: Pilot discovery / k8s config events and any endpoint/EDS issues.
panels.title: Istio Config / Endpoint Events

title: Kubernetes
